code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Strong dissatisfaction or immediate return
    - Safety hazards or failure within 30 days
    - Explicit warnings: "dangerous," "don't buy," "returned immediately"
    - Pattern: "Broke within [timeframe]" + negative conclusion

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Core functionality failure despite partial utility
    - Multiple defects requiring workarounds
    - Regret language: "waste of money," "false advertising"
    - Example: "Works BUT [critical flaw makes it unusable]"

    3 Stars (Neutral / Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with conditional utility
    - Temporary solution: "better than nothing"
    - Non-essential flaws with basic functionality intact
    - Requires significant user effort for basic function

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Explicit star deduction for specific usability issues
    - Functional excellence with minor convenience drawbacks
    - Example: "Deduct 1 star for X but still works well"
    - Maintains recommendation despite clear flaws

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - Explicit "5 stars overall" declarations override aspect critiques
    - Multi-feature praise outweighing single drawback
    - Deducted stars with strong recommendation →5 ("Deduct 1 star but still love")
    - Transformative benefits: "life-changing," "exceeded expectations"
    - Price praise + "worth every penny" without functional complaints

    EDGE CASE HANDLING:
    - Final assessment rule: Last substantive statement dictates rating
    - Core functionality excellent + minor usability →4 unless strong endorsement
    - Safety/return mentions →1 regardless of other positives
    - Multi-update reviews: Prioritize most recent experience
    - Practical utility praised despite single-use nature →5
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1 (5→4 Fix):
Review: "Over the past two months I've gotten numerous kitchen towels. Try them, wash with softener... still keep at least four."
<Reasoning>Repeated attempts to achieve functionality indicate unresolved issues despite persistence.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 2 (Key 4→5 Case):
Review: "Gets 4 stars for comfort but 5 overall - 3 amazing features make it exceptional!"
<Reasoning>Explicit overall rating override via multi-feature praise despite specific critique.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 3 (New 5-Star Pattern):
Review: "Deducting one star for lid design, but this is revolutionary! Changed how I cook."
<Reasoning>Strong transformative praise outweighs minor critique through enthusiastic endorsement.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 4 (Durability 4-Star):
Review: "Love these towels! Deducting one star - lint issues but durability impresses."
<Reasoning>Specific flaw balanced by praised longevity. Maintains recommendation.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 5 (5→4 Error Fix):
Review: "SOLVES my scraping needs perfectly! Deducting one star because not dishwasher safe."
<Reasoning>Explicit deduction for specific usability flaw without overriding endorsement.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 6 (Core Failure 2-Star):
Review: "Blade rusted after 2 uses. Makes great smoothies when it works."
<Reasoning>Core functionality compromised by material defect despite temporary utility.</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 7 (Explicit 5-Star):
Review: "Not dishwasher safe but STILL 5 stars - best scraper ever!"
<Reasoning>Clear 5-star declaration overrides convenience flaw through strong endorsement.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 8 (Safety 1-Star):
Review: "Smoked upon arrival - immediate fire hazard! Return initiated."
<Reasoning>Safety hazard overrides all other considerations regardless of any potential positives.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 9 (Transformative 5-Star):
Review: "Revolutionized my kitchen! Minor size quibble irrelevant to performance."
<Reasoning>Transformative praise makes trivial drawback negligible through enthusiastic endorsement.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (32 total misclassified):
  gold 5 -> 4: 12
  gold 4 -> 5: 9
  gold 4 -> 3: 4
  gold 1 -> 2: 4
  gold 1 -> 4: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 2, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "The towels are definitely cute and well made. Very attractive. The only reason Im not giving them five stars is because they aren't very absorbent. Out of the package they aren't really absorbent at all. Throw them in the wash with some fabric soften..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 3, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "I really like these potholders. They look great and do a good job of keeping hot pots and pans and other dishes out of the oven off the table and counters. They had a bit of a plastic smell when they arrived but after I washed them (they held up fine..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "I bought these mainly for sending leftovers. It is easier than sending my rubbermaid containers because I am not out much money if they don't come back."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "My wife and I recently got talked into a camping weekend, and as neither of us has camped in decades, we wanted to do so in a bit of luxury.  I'm a rather large dude, and I've laid on enough beds that lost all of their air to know I wanted a good bed..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "There is only one problem for the experienced cook when it comes to this set - you're likely to already have one or more of these tools and possibly Oxo Good Grips at that! That's why I think this set is best suited for someone setting up a kitchen f..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [3, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "Great size, convenient for one-skillet meals."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 5] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "Grunds bathmat is 100% certified organic cotton  with the main section being long straight cotton fibers and it has three stripes of loops on each end. This fiber is not treated with bleach, formaldehyde,  or any other harmful toxic substances. This ..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 2] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "Over the past two months or so I've gotten numerous sets of kitchen towels and the process was always the same. Try them. See the weren't absorbent. Wash them numerous times with fabric softener to make them somewhat absorbent and keep at least four ..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "I like this towel. It is 100% Turkish cotton and measures 27 inches by 55 inches. The dobby weave is somewhat intricate so you are going to get some good absorbency. This towel also comes in a bath sheet, which is competively priced with the local st..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 32 errors, 19 borderline out of 80 examples."}
